ingredients.name: Takeshita Street Entrance Arch (Harajuku Station)
ingredients.localName: 竹下通り 入口アーチ (原宿駅)
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-19-11 Jingumae, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3, Nobara Kugisaki walks through this iconic entrance arch on her very first visit to Tokyo, browsing the trendy shops and fashion stalls of Takeshita Street before the trio receives their first real mission.

ingredients.name: Gusto Shinjuku Yasukuni-dori Store
ingredients.localName: ガスト 新宿靖国通店
ingredients.type: ファミリー レストラン
categories: ファミリー レストラン
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-17 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 5, Suguru Geto meets with the cursed spirits Jogo, Hanami, and Dagon near this family restaurant on Yasukuni-dori. In the anime the restaurant is renamed "Rice," but the building facade is directly based on this Gusto branch, where the villains conspire about using cursed spirits against sorcerers.

ingredients.name: Fuji Coin Lockers K-2340
ingredients.address: 3F, 1 Chome-14-2 Jingumae, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3, Nobara remembers mid-shopping that she needs to retrieve her belongings from coin lockers near Harajuku. This locker spot is where the trio gathers before Yuji and Nobara bond over her no-nonsense personality during her Tokyo debut.

ingredients.name: Daikoku Sushi
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-2-6 Shinmachi, Aomori, 030-0801, Japan

ingredients.name: City House Yoyogi
ingredients.address: シティハウス代々木, 1 Chome-31-8 Yoyogi, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0053, Japan

ingredients.name: Caffè Veloce - Nishi-Shinjuku
ingredients.localName: カフェ・ベローチェ 西新宿店
ingredients.address: 加賀谷ビル 1F, 7 Chome-10-7 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan

ingredients.name: Asagaya Station
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-36-2 Asagayaminami, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Asagaya Station serves as the transit hub for the Asagaya district featured in Episode 23, where the surrounding shopping streets and cafes are used to depict everyday Tokyo life in the series.

ingredients.name: Asagaya Pearl Center Shopping Street
ingredients.localName: 阿佐谷パールセンター 商店街
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-36-7 Asagayaminami, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This covered shotengai arcade near Asagaya Station is depicted in Episode 23 as part of the everyday Tokyo neighborhood backdrop, bringing the grounded, lived-in feel of the city that defines Jujutsu Kaisen's setting outside the curse battles.

ingredients.name: AOKI Kichijoji Sun Road Ten
ingredients.localName: AOKI 吉祥寺サンロード店
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-13-1 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located on Kichijoji's Sun Road shopping arcade, this clothing store appears in the Kichijoji arc of Episode 8, the same neighborhood where three students are found dead near the Kinema Cinema, drawing Nanami and Itadori into a gruesome investigation.

ingredients.name: Yoyogi Station
ingredients.address: 1-chōme-34 Yoyogi, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0053, Japan

ingredients.name: Yagiyama Bridge Tatsunoguchi Valley
ingredients.address: Taihaku Ward, Sendai, Miyagi 982-0837, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge in Sendai is the real-world model for the Yasohachi Bridge in Episode 22, where a dangerous curse lurks beneath that kills anyone who ventures under it. In the anime the location is set in Saitama, but the visual reference comes directly from this Sendai span, making it a notable pilgrimage spot for fans of the Yasohachi Bridge curse arc.

ingredients.name: World Business Garden No. 2 Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: ワールドビジネスガーデン 第2駐車場
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-6-1 Nakase, Mihama Ward, Chiba, 261-0023, Japan

ingredients.name: Ueno Zoo
ingredients.address: 9-83 Uenokoen, Taito City, Tokyo 110-8711, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ueno Zoo and the surrounding Ueno Park area appear in the opening theme of Jujutsu Kaisen, contributing to the series' visually rich montage of Tokyo's diverse districts. Ueno Park itself carries strong historical resonance as a site associated with the restless dead, fitting the tone of the series.

ingredients.name: Tsubojiri Station
ingredients.address: Tatsutani Ikedachō Nishiyama, Miyoshi, Tokushima 778-0040, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This remote, unstaffed station deep in the mountains of Tokushima appears in the opening theme of Jujutsu Kaisen in a scene believed to depict a young Nobara Kugisaki with her childhood friend Saori. Its isolated, atmospheric platform surrounded by wild mountains perfectly captures Nobara's rural backstory before she moves to Tokyo.

ingredients.name: The Industry Club of Japan
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-4-6 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0005, Japan

ingredients.name: Subaru Building Shinjuku Eye
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-7-2 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This large yellow eye sculpture on the Subaru Building in Nishi-Shinjuku is where Aoi Todo strikes his signature pose in the opening theme of Jujutsu Kaisen Season 1. The iconic shot of Todo in front of the eye has become one of the most recognizable pilgrimage frames from the opening.

ingredients.name: Starbucks Coffee - Keio Shinjuku 3-chome Building
ingredients.localName: スターバックス コーヒー 京王新宿三丁目ビル店
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
ingredients.address: 京王新宿三丁目ビル, 3 Chome-1-24 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Sendagaya Tunnel
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒151-0051 Tokyo, Shibuya, Sendagaya, 2 Chome−24−1
ingredients.entityContext: The Sendagaya Tunnel opens the first Season 1 opening sequence with Megumi Fushiguro crouching on the floor, investigating something unseen. Built in 1964 and passing beneath a cemetery, this short 61-meter tunnel is widely regarded as one of Tokyo's most haunted spots, making it a perfect location to establish the anime's eerie supernatural atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Saint Marc Cafe - Asagaya Station
ingredients.localName: サンマルクカフェ 阿佐ヶ谷駅前店
ingredients.address: ＡＳＡＧＡＹＡ南口駅前ビル １階, 2 Chome-15-2 Asagayaminami, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0004, Japan

ingredients.name: Ryuseido Pharmacy Shinjuku Shop
ingredients.localName: 龍生堂薬局 新宿店
ingredients.address: 龍生堂ビル, 3 Chome-21-6 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 5, the cursed spirits including Geto, Jogo, and Hanami cross the pedestrian crossing directly in front of this pharmacy on Shinjuku's Kabukicho border. The building facade is a confirmed real-world match, and the crossing scene marks the moment the curse alliance begins plotting against jujutsu sorcerers.

ingredients.name: Roppongi Sta.
ingredients.address: 9 Chome-7-39 Akasaka, Minato City, Tokyo 107-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Roppongi Station is the entry point for Episode 3's mission, where Yuji, Megumi, and Nobara travel to Roppongi to exorcise a curse from an abandoned building. The Roppongi district and its nightlife environment set the tone for the trio's first team operation.

ingredients.name: Roppongi Keyakizaka Dori
ingredients.address: 6-chōme Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Keyakizaka Street in Roppongi, lined with zelkova trees and upscale shops, appears in Episode 3 as part of the Roppongi district setting where the first-years take on a curse mission. The street's sleek, affluent character contrasts sharply with the supernatural danger lurking nearby.

ingredients.name: Roppongi Hills
ingredients.address: 6 Chome-10-1 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-6108, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Roppongi Hills appears in the opening theme of Jujutsu Kaisen Season 1, where Nobara is shown against this landmark complex. The Mori Garden side of Roppongi Hills also features in the opening sequence alongside the nearby tunnel, establishing Roppongi as one of the show's signature Tokyo backdrops.

ingredients.name: Roppingi Hills - Tokyo City View
ingredients.localName: 六本木ヒルズ展望台 東京シティビュー
ingredients.address: Roppongi Hills Mori Tower, 52階, 6 Chome-10-1 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The observation deck on the 52nd floor of Mori Tower in Roppongi Hills is featured in Episode 3, offering a sweeping view of Tokyo that appears during the first-years' Roppongi mission. The dramatic city panorama from this deck underscores the contrast between Tokyo's glamorous skyline and the cursed dangers hidden within it.

ingredients.name: Otaguro Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-33-12 Ogikubo, Suginami City, Tokyo 167-0051, Japan

ingredients.name: Onda Bridge ruins
ingredients.address: 5 Chome-11 Jingumae, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Noge 326
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒231-0065 Kanagawa, Yokohama, Naka Ward, Miyagawacho, 2 Chome−19-2 3階

ingredients.name: Nicolai Bergmann Flowers & Design Flagship Store
ingredients.address: 5 Chome-7-2 Minamiaoyama, Minato City, Tokyo 107-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The cafe attached to this Minami-Aoyama flower design flagship is where Kento Nanami is depicted taking a quiet break in the opening theme of Jujutsu Kaisen. The Danish-influenced space, favored by the half-Danish Nanami, became a fan pilgrimage spot, though the round table shown in the opening has since been removed.

ingredients.name: Mister Donut Kichijoji Sun Road shop
ingredients.localName: ミスタードーナツ 吉祥寺サンロードショップ
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-11-28 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located on Kichijoji's Sun Road arcade, this Mister Donut branch is part of the Kichijoji area featured in Episodes 8 and 9 during Nanami and Itadori's investigation into a string of deaths tied to a curse at a nearby cinema. The familiar chain restaurant setting grounds the supernatural horror in everyday Tokyo life.

ingredients.name: Makuhari Messe International Exhibition Hall 9-11
ingredients.localName: 幕張メッセ 国際展示場 展示ホール9～11
ingredients.type: コンベンション センター
categories: コンベンション センター
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-２-1 Nakase, Mihama Ward, Chiba, 261-8550, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Makuhari Messe, one of Japan's largest convention and exhibition centers, is the real-world model for the idol handshake event venue in Episode 8 where Aoi Todo meets his favorite idol Takada-chan. The post-credits scene of Mai attending this event in disguise is a fan-favorite comedic moment tied directly to this Chiba convention complex.

ingredients.name: Makuhari Messe International Exhibition Hall 1-3
ingredients.localName: 幕張メッセ 国際展示場 1-3ホール
ingredients.type: コンベンション センター
categories: コンベンション センター
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-１ Nakase, Mihama Ward, Chiba, 261-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: These halls of Makuhari Messe form part of the convention center complex used as the model for Takada-chan's idol handshake event in Episode 8. Todo's obsession with Takada-chan is one of the series' most beloved running gags, making Makuhari Messe a memorable reference point for JJK fans.

ingredients.name: Kiyomizu-dera
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-294 Kiyomizu, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0862, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kiyomizudera's iconic wooden stage and surrounding Kyoto temple grounds serve as the visual reference for Kyoto Jujutsu High's environment during the Kyoto Sister-School Goodwill Event arc beginning in Episode 8. The ancient temple's atmosphere, described in the series as jujutsu's sacred land, is a fitting backdrop for the fierce inter-school battle between Tokyo and Kyoto sorcerers.

ingredients.name: Kichijoji Plaza
ingredients.address: atré Kichijoji, アトレ吉祥寺 B1F, 1 Chome-1-24 Kichijoji Minamicho, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kichijoji Plaza is adjacent to the Kinema Cinema movie theater, the central location in Episodes 8 and 9 where three students are found horribly mutated after a curse encounter. Nanami and Itadori investigate this Kichijoji cinema complex, leading to Itadori's first battle alongside Junpei Yoshino.

ingredients.name: Jeans Mate Takeshita Street Store
ingredients.localName: ジーンズメイト 竹下通り店
ingredients.address: 柴崎ビル 1F, 1 Chome-20-9 Jingumae, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This clothing store on Takeshita Street is part of the Episode 3 Harajuku sequence where Nobara explores the street during her first day in Tokyo. The lively, fashion-forward atmosphere of Takeshita Street, including shops like this one, captures Nobara's excitement and culture-shock as a newcomer to the city.

ingredients.name: Harajuku Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome Jingumae, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Harajuku Station is where Nobara Kugisaki arrives in Episode 3 on her first day in Tokyo, heading straight to the nearby Takeshita Street. The station and its immediate surroundings appear as Nobara meets up with Yuji and Megumi before the trio embarks on their first Roppongi mission.

ingredients.name: HUB Kichijoji South Exit
ingredients.address: Ｂ１Ｆ, 1 Chome-5-7 Kichijoji Minamicho, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0003, Japan
